message:
test: add UI hook, primitive, cost, and recommendation tests (bd-tr6k, bd-3eek, bd-2pim)

- 4 hook tests: useInView, useCountUp, useMousePosition, useReducedMotion
- 7 UI primitive tests: skip-link, live-region, error-boundary, label, select, sheet, switch
- Expanded cost.test.ts (4→28 tests) and recommendations.test.ts (4→36 tests)
- Closed bd-vvq4 (CLI core commands already had tests)
- 97 web test files (2480 tests), 411 core tests, all passing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

APPROACH: Create a test-utils/fetch-fixtures.ts with realistic API response shapes. Reduce mock boilerplate — hooks should be tested against consistent, realistic data shapes, not ad-hoc fakes per test. BLOCKED BY bd-tr6k — need hook testing patterns established first.","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:32:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1p9c","title":"web/components: transcript component tests (timeline, enhanced-timeline, message-content, message-detail, stats-dashboard, insight-card, etc.)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:28:31.851373478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T00:31:44.113573206Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":65,"issue_id":"bd-1p9c","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SCOPE: Test apps/web/src/components/transcript/ (~12 files: timeline, enhanced-timeline, message-content, message-detail, stats-dashboard, insight-card, annotated-guide, enhanced-hero, enhanced-stats, multi-model-feedback, timeline-skeleton). APPROACH: Provide real transcript data fixtures, render components, verify structure. Complex but testable — test with realistic session data.","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:31:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ppu","title":"Backend: Budget persistence + hard stop enforcement (Premium)","description":"Implement premium backend storage + enforcement for budget settings. Scope: API endpoints to read/write budget caps + alerts, persist per-user settings, enforce hard-stop on overages for prompt runs, and expose audit log for alerts. Mirror bead in premium repo and link cross-repo dependencies.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:04:43.856815347Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:05:31.256945535Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:05:31.256921389Z","close_reason":"Spec complete in this repo; backend persistence/enforcement must be implemented in premium repo. Public repo follow-ups (CLI/web consuming /cli/budgets when available) should be tracked separately once endpoints land.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":19,"issue_id":"bd-1ppu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Draft backend spec for budgets/hard stop (premium repo):

Data model:
- budgets: user_id (pk), monthly_cap_usd, per_run_cap_usd, alerts_enabled, hard_stop_enabled, created_at, updated_at.
- budget_alerts: id, user_id, alert_type (per_run|monthly|hard_stop), cap_usd, total_cost_usd, currency, prompt_id, prompt_title, model, input_tokens, output_tokens, source (estimate|actual), created_at.
- usage_rollups: user_id, month (YYYY-MM), total_cost_usd, total_tokens, last_run_at.

API (premium):
- GET /cli/budgets -> { budgets, usage_month, usage, alerts_enabled, hard_stop_enabled }.
- PUT /cli/budgets -> update caps/toggles; return saved payload.
- GET /cli/budget-alerts?limit=&before= -> list alerts for UI/CLI.
- POST /cli/budget-alerts -> optional client-side logging for cost estimates (optional).

Enforcement:
- On any prompt run/execute endpoint, compute projected cost. If hard_stop_enabled and projected monthly total > cap -> return 402/403 with code budget_exceeded and include caps + current usage.
- If per_run cap exceeded, allow run but record alert (and return warning in response).
- Monthly usage should be computed from actual usage only (estimates should not increment usage).

Client integration notes (public repo):
- CLI currently logs alerts locally to ~/.config/jfp/budget-alerts.jsonl.
- Once backend exists, update CLI status to pull budgets/usage from /cli/budgets for premium users; local config remains fallback.
- Web budget settings page is local-only; wire to backend when endpoints exist.","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:10:03Z"},{"id":20,"issue_id":"bd-1ppu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Additional spec details:

Response shapes:
- GET /cli/budgets -> { ok: true, data: { budgets: { monthlyCapUsd, perRunCapUsd, alertsEnabled, hardStopEnabled }, usage: { month, totalCostUsd, totalTokens, lastRunAt } } }
- PUT /cli/budgets (body: partial budgets) -> same payload as GET.
- Error (budget hard stop): status 402 or 403 with { ok:false, error:{ code:"budget_exceeded", message, caps:{ monthlyCapUsd, perRunCapUsd }, usage:{ month, totalCostUsd }, projected:{ totalCostUsd, inputTokens, outputTokens, model } } }.
- Warning only (per-run over cap, hard stop disabled): include { warning:{ code:"budget_warning", message, capUsd, totalCostUsd } } in normal response.

Rollups:
- usage_rollups should be updated on actual billable runs only.
- Use UTC month boundaries; month stored as YYYY-MM string for deterministic queries.

Client expectations:
- CLI/web should treat missing /cli/budgets endpoint as "not available" and keep local-only UI/alerts.
- CLI can show budget status in `jfp status` only when endpoint returns data; otherwise fallback to config-only summary.","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:17:00Z"},{"id":22,"issue_id":"bd-1ppu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"bv triage 2026-02-01: all open beads already in_progress; continuing bd-1ppu. Remaining work is premium-repo implementation (endpoints + persistence + enforcement). Blocked here until premium repo mirror/assignment. Suggested next steps: mirror bead in premium repo; implement /cli/budgets + /cli/budget-alerts; enforce hard-stop on run endpoints; add usage rollups + tests.","created_at":"2026-02-01T22:57:22Z"},{"id":24,"issue_id":"bd-1ppu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"bv triage 2026-02-01T23:23Z: top picks unchanged; all open beads already in_progress. Continuing bd-1ppu.

Concrete next steps (premium repo):
1) Add budgets table + budget_alerts + usage_rollups (UTC month key).
2) Implement GET/PUT /cli/budgets (auth + validate, return usage).
3) Implement GET /cli/budget-alerts (paging by before/limit).
4) Enforce hard stop in run/execute endpoints; return error code budget_exceeded with caps + usage + projected cost.
5) Add warning payload for per-run overages when hard stop disabled.
6) Tests: budget validation, rollups increment on actual usage, hard-stop block, warning path.

Public repo follow-up (after premium endpoints exist):
- Update CLI status to prefer /cli/budgets (fallback to local config).
- Wire web Budget Settings page to backend instead of localStorage; keep local-only fallback if endpoint missing.","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:24:14Z"}]}

Need to: fix deps, integrate logging, verify ALL specs pass, add CLI E2E tests, create CI pipeline, add production smoke tests. 10 subtasks with critical path: fix deps → verify specs → CI → production.","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:32:58Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2txr","title":"E2E Tests: History UI Components","description":"## Task
Add UI-level E2E tests for the Recently Viewed History feature.

## Context
The API-level E2E tests exist (e2e/history/recently-viewed.spec.ts) but UI component tests are missing.

## Test Scenarios
1. **RecentlyViewedSidebar Component**
   - Sidebar displays recent items
   - Clear history button works
   - Items link to correct pages
   - Empty state displays correctly
   - Loading state shows correctly

2. **History Page (/history)**
   - Page loads and displays items
   - Search/filter works
   - Clear all history works
   - Timestamps display correctly
   - Links navigate correctly

3. **History Tracking Integration**
   - Viewing a prompt adds to history
   - Search queries are tracked
   - Deduplication prevents rapid duplicates
   - Items appear in sidebar after viewing

## Files to Modify
- e2e/history/recently-viewed.spec.ts (add UI tests)
- e2e/lib/history-helpers.ts (add UI selectors)

## Success Criteria
- All sidebar interactions tested
- History page fully tested
- Integration with tracking verified","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralStar","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:45:40.323914674Z","created_by":"CoralStar","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:46.005730906Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:46.005649042Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":13,"issue_id":"bd-2txr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation complete:
- Added 16 new UI tests (34 total including API tests)
- History Page UI tests: header, empty state, items display, search filter, clear button, navigation
- RecentlyViewedSidebar tests: title, empty state, history link, items display, 8-item limit
- History Tracking Integration tests: cross-page sync, clear sync, search query display
- Updated history-helpers.ts with better UI selectors for sidebar and history page
- 68 total tests (34 × 2 browser configs: chromium + Mobile Chrome)","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vp1","title":"Bound share verify rate-limit bucket map size","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-09T05:20:38.438057764Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:29:49.892528794Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:29:49.892481957Z","close_reason":"Completed: migrated /api/share/[code]/verify from unbounded route-local map to shared bounded rate limiter with tests and full quality-gate validation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2vsu","title":"Feature: Personalized Recommendations Feed (Pro)","description":"## Background
Pro users want to discover high‑value prompts quickly without browsing the entire catalog. A personalized feed drives engagement and retention.

## Scope
- "For You" feed based on usage signals (views, runs, saves) + explicit preferences.
- Explanations: "Because you used X" or "Trending in your category".
- Weekly digest email (opt‑in) with top recommendations.
- CLI: `jfp recommend` returns a ranked list with rationale.
- Prompts/prompt packs only (no SKILL.md or skills content).

## Deliverables
- Ranking pipeline and data model for signals.
- Feed UI with filters (timeframe, category).
- Digest email template + scheduling job.
- CLI command with JSON output.

## Test Requirements
### Unit Tests
- Ranking weight calculations and deterministic ordering.
- Preference filtering and exclusion rules.

### Integration Tests
- Signal ingestion -> feed updates -> UI reflects changes.
- Digest job generates consistent results.

### E2E Tests
- Toggle preferences -> feed updates -> digest preview.
- CLI recommend -> matches feed top items.

## Success Criteria
- Users find relevant prompts faster.
- Feed is explainable and feels trustworthy.
- Opt‑in privacy settings are respected.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"VioletWolf","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:14:02.330248162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:07.759108222Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:07.759072725Z","close_reason":"Completed OSS slice: core recommendation engine (signals + preferences) + web For You feed (/for-you) + /settings/recommendations prefs + CLI jfp recommend. Premium sync/persistence + any digest email job live in premium repo.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":34,"issue_id":"bd-2vsu","author":"VioletWolf","text":"Starting web-side unblocked slice of bd-2vsu: add explicit recommendation preferences (category boost + category hide) stored device-locally (localStorage key jfp_recommendation_preferences_v1). New Settings entry: /settings/recommendations, and For You section consumes preferences via getForYouRecommendations(). For You remains hidden when there is no activity AND no preferences (to avoid duplicating Featured), but will show once preferences are set even with zero history.","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:50:18Z"}]}

-{"id":"bd-2yxy","title":"core: search engine unit tests (engine.ts, bm25.ts, tokenize.ts, hash-embedder.ts, metadata.ts)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:27:51.194445494Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T00:45:09.358264809Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":45,"issue_id":"bd-2yxy","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SCOPE: Test packages/core/src/search/ (engine.ts, bm25.ts, tokenize.ts, hash-embedder.ts, metadata.ts).  {"id":"bd-32rw","title":"Fix flakey CLI test timeout for deprecated skills commands","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-06T16:46:04.470668197Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T16:51:55.467931042Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T16:51:55.467905073Z","close_reason":"Raised CLI suite per-test timeout to match spawnJfp and prevent SIGTERM flakes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":37,"issue_id":"bd-32rw","author":"QuietBasin","text":"Root cause: bun test default per-test timeout is 5000ms, but our CLI E2E helpers spawn bun run jfp.ts with 15-30s timeouts. Fix: raise CLI suite timeout via test:cli/test:cli:coverage scripts (--timeout=30000) and add an explicit per-test timeout in packages/cli/__tests__/commands/skills.test.ts for extra robustness.","created_at":"2026-02-06T16:51:35Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3jtd","title":"EPIC: World-Class UI/UX Polish - Mobile & Desktop Optimization","description":"## Overview
Hyper-optimize both mobile and desktop UI/UX to achieve world-class visual appeal, polish, and slickness following best practices from industry leaders like Stripe.

## Completed Work
1. ✅ Fixed critical mobile button text truncation in PromptDetailModal
   - Restructured to mobile-first approach with full-width primary action
   - Secondary actions in 2x2 grid that never truncates

## Mobile Optimization Goals
- Ensure all touch targets are 44px minimum (iOS HIG standard)
- Proper safe area insets for notched devices
- Bottom-sheet interactions for modals
- Optimized category filter horizontal scrolling
- Touch-friendly gestures with haptic feedback

## Desktop Optimization Goals
- Refined hover states and micro-interactions
- Proper keyboard navigation
- Consistent typography and spacing
- Premium shadow and elevation design
- Stripe-level polish on all interactive elements

## Components to Audit
- [x] PromptDetailModal - Fixed
- [x] Button component - Already well-designed
- [x] BottomSheet - Well-designed with safe area support
- [x] SpotlightSearch - Well-designed with mobile-first approach
- [x] Nav - Good touch targets
- [x] BottomNav - Good mobile design
- [x] PromptCard - Good Stripe-inspired design

## Status
Initial critical bug fixed. Full audit shows components are already well-designed following mobile-first principles.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-21T20:27:47.367791503Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T20:28:41.377830454Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T20:28:41.377778706Z","close_reason":"Completed UI/UX audit.  {"id":"bd-wsye","title":"Docs: Clarify CLI Free vs Pro + Build Guidance","description":"## Background
Users reported that the jfp CLI does not compile and are confused about Free vs Pro gating.

## Scope
- Clarify Free vs Pro command gating in the main README.
- Add build guidance confirming no Pro account is required to compile.
- Update CLI premium integration doc with a gating summary.
- Improve bug report template for build failures.

## Completed Changes
- Updated README Free vs Pro section + build steps.
- Added gating summary to docs/cli-premium-integration.md.
- Added CLI build diagnostics section to .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md.

## Success Criteria
- OSS users can build the CLI without Pro.
- Pro-only commands are clearly communicated.
- Issue reports include build details.
